name: Ploceidae
description: Weavers are a large family of passerine birds known for their elaborately woven nests. They are primarily found in sub-Saharan Africa, with a few species occurring in Asia, and are named for their remarkable nest-building behavior.

children.name: Ploceus
children.description: A large genus of weavers with stout conical bills, known for their bright breeding plumages and woven nests.

children.name: Ploceus cucullatus
children.description: The Village Weaver is a common bird with black head and yellow body in breeding males. It is found across sub-Saharan Africa, building large woven nests in colonies near water.

children.name: Ploceus philippinus
children.description: The Baya Weaver is a sparrow-sized bird with bright yellow crown and breast in breeding males. It is famous for its intricately woven retort-shaped nests, built in colonies across South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Ploceus velatus
children.description: The Southern Masked Weaver has a black face and throat with bright yellow body in breeding males. It is common across southern Africa, weaving spherical nests in trees and reed beds.

children.name: Ploceus spekei
children.description: Speke's Weaver is a yellow weaver with a black face, throat, and bill in breeding males. It is endemic to the Horn of Africa, inhabiting savannas and building woven nests in acacia trees.

children.name: Ploceus nigerrimus
children.description: Vieillot's Black Weaver is a sexually dimorphic species: the male is glossy black with a pale eye, while the female is olive-green with streaked underparts. It inhabits forests and woodlands across equatorial Africa.

children.name: Ploceus nigricollis
children.description: The Black-necked Weaver has a black throat and nape, bright yellow body, and olive-green crown. It is found in woodlands and forest edges across much of sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Ploceus subaureus
children.description: The Golden Weaver is a bright yellow bird with an orange-red eye and olive-tinted back. It inhabits coastal wetlands and riverine vegetation of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Ploceus xanthops
children.description: Holub's Golden Weaver is a large, heavy-billed weaver with bright yellow plumage and a greenish wash on the back. It is found in marshes and moist grasslands of central and southern Africa.

children.name: Ploceus aurantius
children.description: The Orange Weaver has a brilliant orange-yellow body with a black face and throat in breeding males. It frequents coastal mangroves and swamps of western and central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus rubiginosus
children.description: The Chestnut Weaver has a rich chestnut body with a black face and dark eye in breeding males. It inhabits dry savannas and scrublands of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Ploceus intermedius
children.description: The Lesser Masked Weaver has a black face mask, bright yellow body, and pale eye in breeding males. It is a colonial nester found in woodlands and savannas of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Ploceus baglafecht
children.description: A variable weaver with a yellow body, black mask, and olive crown in most subspecies. It is found across East and Central Africa in forests, woodlands, and gardens.

children.name: Ploceus castanops
children.description: A weaver with a brown throat, yellow body, and olive crown. It is found in swamps and wetlands of East Africa from Uganda to Tanzania.

children.name: Ploceus xanthopterus
children.description: A weaver with a brown throat, bright yellow body, and olive-green crown. It is found in wetland habitats of southern and central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus pelzelni
children.description: A small weaver with a slender bill, yellow body, and black face mask. It is found in marshes and reed beds of western and central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus preussi
children.description: A weaver with a yellow body, black crown and face, and olive back. It is found in the mountains of Cameroon and adjacent Nigeria.

children.name: Ploceus dorsomaculatus
children.description: A weaver with a yellow crown, black face, olive upperparts, and yellow underparts. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus olivaceiceps
children.description: A weaver with an olive-green head, yellow underparts, and yellow-green upperparts. It is found in forests and woodlands of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Ploceus nicolli
children.description: A weaver with a yellow body, black crown, and olive-green back. It is endemic to the Usambara and Uluguru Mountains of Tanzania.

children.name: Ploceus melanocephalus
children.description: A weaver with a black head, bright yellow body, and olive-green back. It is found in wetlands and moist savannas of western and central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus capensis
children.description: A large weaver with a long bill, yellow body, and olive back. The male has a rufous wash on the face in breeding plumage. It is endemic to South Africa.

children.name: Ploceus bertrandi
children.description: A weaver with a black head, chestnut breast band, yellow belly, and olive back. It is found in forests and woodlands of East Africa.

children.name: Ploceus insignis
children.description: A weaver with a brown cap, yellow underparts, black face, and olive upperparts. It is found in montane forests of central and East Africa.

children.name: Ploceus luchsingeri
children.description: A weaver with a yellow body, black face mask, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus ocularis
children.description: A weaver with bright yellow underparts, olive back, and a distinctive yellow eye-ring. It is found in forests and woodlands across central and East Africa.

children.name: Ploceus albinucha
children.description: A weaver with a white nape patch, black head, yellow body, and olive-green back. It is found in lowland forests of western and central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus megarhynchus
children.description: A weaver with a yellow throat and breast, black face, and brown upperparts. It is found in wetlands of the eastern Himalayas and northern Myanmar.

children.name: Ploceus manyar
children.description: A weaver with a streaked yellow and brown body, pale bill, and a partial black hood. It is found in wetlands and rice paddies of South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Ploceus benghalensis
children.description: A weaver with a black breast, yellow crown, and streaked brown back. It is found in grasslands and marshes of the Indian subcontinent.

children.name: Ploceus manucerus
children.description: A yellow weaver with an olive-green back and black bill. It is found in lowland forests and clearings of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus taeniopterus
children.description: A weaver with a black face mask, bright yellow body, and olive back. It is found in dry savannas of the Sahel region from Senegal to Sudan.

children.name: Ploceus granulifrons
children.description: A weaver with a yellow body, black face, and a distinctive granular texture on the forehead. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus heuglini
children.description: A weaver with a black face mask, bright yellow body, and olive back. It is found in savannas of eastern and central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus waldeni
children.description: A weaver with a yellow body, black crown, and olive upperparts. It is endemic to the island of Principe in the Gulf of Guinea.

children.name: Ploceus bahni
children.description: A weaver with a yellow body, black face, and olive upperparts. It is found in montane forests of Cameroon and Nigeria.

children.name: Anomalospiza
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Cuckoo Finch, a brood parasite that lays its eggs in the nests of cisticolas and prinias. It has a stout, conical bill and resembles a small weaver in appearance.

children.name: Anomalospiza imberbis
children.description: A small weaver-like finch with a stout bill, yellow underparts in breeding males, and streaked brown upperparts. It is a brood parasite, laying its eggs in the nests of small warblers across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Quelea
children.description: A genus of small weavers that form some of the largest bird flocks on Earth.

children.name: Quelea quelea
children.description: The Red-billed Quelea is a small weaver with a distinctive red bill and variable breeding plumage. It forms enormous flocks that can number in the millions, and is considered a major agricultural pest in Africa.

children.name: Quelea erythrops
children.description: The Red-headed Quelea has a bright red head and breast in breeding males, with a brown body and pale bill. It inhabits moist savannas and grasslands of western and central Africa.

children.name: Quelea cardinalis
children.description: The Cardinal Quelea has a rich crimson-red head and breast, brown back, and pinkish-red bill in breeding males. It is found in grassy wetlands of eastern and central Africa.

children.name: Amblyospiza
children.description: A monotypic genus of large, thick-billed weaver found in African wetlands and forests.

children.name: Amblyospiza albifrons
children.description: The Grosbeak Weaver is a large, robust weaver with a massive conical bill and a white forehead patch. It inhabits reed beds and forest edges across sub-Saharan Africa, building large woven nests.

children.name: Sporopipes
children.description: A small genus of small, finely patterned weavers with scalloped or spotted plumage. They are found in dry savannas and scrublands of Africa, building spherical grass nests in trees and bushes.

children.name: Sporopipes frontalis
children.description: A small weaver with a spotted forehead and crown, brown upperparts with fine barring, and pale underparts. It is found in dry savannas of the Sahel region.

children.name: Sporopipes squamifrons
children.description: A small weaver with a scaly-patterned forehead, brown upperparts, and pale underparts. It is found in dry scrub and savannas of southern Africa.

children.name: Philetairus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Sociable Weaver, famous for building enormous communal nests that resemble haystacks in trees and on utility poles. These structures are among the largest bird nests in the world and can house hundreds of birds.

children.name: Philetairus socius
children.description: A small, pale weaver with a black chin and bib, brown upperparts with fine barring, and white underparts. It builds massive, permanent communal nests in acacia trees of southern Africa.

children.name: Pseudonigrita
children.description: A small genus of social weavers that build their own nests, distinct from Philetairus but also highly gregarious. They inhabit dry savannas of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Pseudonigrita arnaudi
children.description: A social weaver with a grey crown, black mask, brown upperparts, and white underparts. It builds communal grass nests in acacia trees of East African savannas.

children.name: Pseudonigrita cabanisi
children.description: A social weaver with a black crown, white face, brown upperparts, and white underparts. It is found in dry savannas of the Horn of Africa.

children.name: Malimbus
children.description: A genus of forest-dwelling weavers with bright red and black plumage, found in tropical Africa.

children.name: Malimbus nitens
children.description: The Blue-billed Malimbe has a glossy black body with a bright red crown, throat, and breast, and a distinctive pale blue bill. It inhabits lowland rainforests of western and central Africa.

children.name: Malimbus rubricollis
children.description: The Red-headed Malimbe has a black body with a bright red head, throat, and upper breast. It is a canopy-dwelling weaver of lowland rainforests across equatorial Africa.

children.name: Malimbus scutatus
children.description: A malimbe with a black body, red crown and vent, and a pale blue bill. It is found in lowland forests of West Africa.

children.name: Malimbus erythrogaster
children.description: A malimbe with a black body, red belly and breast, and red crown patch. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Malimbus ibadanensis
children.description: A malimbe with a black body, red crown and breast band, and a pale blue bill. It is endemic to the Ibadan region of Nigeria and is considered endangered.

children.name: Malimbus cassini
children.description: A malimbe with a black body and a bright red crown. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Malimbus racheliae
children.description: A malimbe with a black body, red crown and throat, and yellow-orange breast. It is found in lowland forests of Cameroon and Gabon.

children.name: Malimbus ballmanni
children.description: A malimbe with a black body and bright red crown, throat, and breast. It is endemic to the Gola Forest region of Sierra Leone and Liberia.

children.name: Malimbus malimbicus
children.description: A malimbe with a black body, red crown and throat, and a distinctive crest. It is found in lowland forests of central and West Africa.

children.name: Malimbus coronatus
children.description: A malimbe with a black body and a bright red crown patch. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Bubalornis
children.description: A genus of large, heavy-billed weavers that build massive, untidy stick nests in trees. They are named for their association with African buffalo and other large mammals.

children.name: Bubalornis albirostris
children.description: A large black weaver with a white bill, white wing patches, and white patches on the flanks. It is found in dry savannas of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Bubalornis niger
children.description: A large black weaver with a bright red bill, white wing patches, and white flanks. It is found in dry savannas of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Dinemellia
children.description: A monotypic genus of weaver with a striking white head and black body, found in dry savannas of East Africa. It builds large, thorny stick nests in acacia trees.

children.name: Dinemellia dinemelli
children.description: A striking weaver with a white head and breast, black body, and bright orange-red rump and underparts. It builds large, messy stick nests in acacias of East African savannas.

children.name: Euplectes
children.description: A genus of weavers known as widowbirds and bishop birds, with males exhibiting striking elongated tail feathers and bright breeding plumage.

children.name: Euplectes ardens
children.description: The Red-collared Widowbird has a black body with a vivid red band across the throat and a long, flowing tail in breeding males. It inhabits moist grasslands and marshes of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes macroura
children.description: The Yellow-mantled Widowbird has a black body with a bright yellow mantle and shoulder patch, and a broad fan-shaped tail in breeding males. It is found in wet grasslands of central and eastern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes axillaris
children.description: The Fan-tailed Widowbird has a black body with orange-red shoulder patches and a short, broad tail in breeding males. It is common in marshes and rank grasslands across much of sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Euplectes capensis
children.description: A bishop with a black body, bright yellow shoulder patch and rump, and a short tail. It is found in grasslands and marshes of southern and eastern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes afer
children.description: A small bishop with a bright yellow crown and underparts, black face, and brown back in breeding males. It is found in wetlands and moist grasslands of western and central Africa.

children.name: Euplectes diadematus
children.description: A bishop with a black body and a bright red crown and forehead. It is endemic to the savannas of eastern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes gierowii
children.description: A large bishop with a black body, yellow shoulder patch, and chestnut back in breeding males. It is found in marshes and moist grasslands of central and eastern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes nigroventris
children.description: A small bishop with a black body, bright red lower back and rump, and a short tail. It is endemic to coastal Kenya and Tanzania, including Zanzibar.

children.name: Euplectes albonotatus
children.description: A widowbird with a black body, white wing patches, and a long flowing tail in breeding males. It is found in grasslands and marshes of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes progne
children.description: A spectacular widowbird with an extremely long, flowing tail that can reach 50 cm in length, black body, and red-and-white shoulder patches. It inhabits grasslands of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes hartlaubi
children.description: A widowbird with a black body, orange shoulder patches, and a medium-length tail. It is found in marshes and wetlands of central and eastern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes jacksoni
children.description: A widowbird with a black body, golden shoulder patches, and a distinctive long, curved tail. It is found in highland grasslands of Kenya and Tanzania.

children.name: Euplectes orix
children.description: A bishop with a striking bright red body, black face and belly, and brown wings. It is found in wetlands and grasslands of southern Africa.

children.name: Euplectes hordeaceus
children.description: A bishop with a bright red body, black wings and face, and brown tail. It is found in wetlands and grasslands of western and central Africa.

children.name: Euplectes psammocromius
children.description: A widowbird with a black body, red shoulder patch, and a long tail. It is found in high-altitude grasslands of eastern Africa.

children.name: Plocepasser
children.description: A genus of social weavers that build large, compartmentalized communal nests, found in savannas of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Plocepasser mahali
children.description: The White-browed Sparrow-Weaver has a brown body, white eyebrow, and white wing patches. It builds large, messy communal nests of grass in acacia trees, occupied year-round by social groups.

children.name: Plocepasser superciliosus
children.description: A sparrow-weaver with a chestnut crown, white eyebrow, brown upperparts, and white underparts. It is found in savannas of eastern Africa.

children.name: Plocepasser donaldsoni
children.description: A sparrow-weaver with a brown cap, white eyebrow, grey-brown upperparts, and white underparts. It is endemic to the dry savannas of the Horn of Africa.

children.name: Plocepasser ruficeps
children.description: A sparrow-weaver with a rufous crown, white eyebrow, brown upperparts, and white underparts. It is found in dry savannas of southern Africa.

children.name: Histurgops
children.description: A monotypic genus of large weaver with a distinctive rufous tail and heavily streaked plumage, endemic to Tanzania. It is a social species that builds messy nests in trees.

children.name: Histurgops crassirostris
children.description: A large weaver with a thick bill, streaked brown upperparts, white underparts, and a bright rufous tail. It is endemic to the savannas of central Tanzania.

children.name: Foudia
children.description: A genus of weavers endemic to the Indian Ocean islands of Madagascar, the Comoros, and the Seychelles. They are sometimes called fodies and are known for their bright red breeding plumage.

children.name: Foudia madagascariensis
children.description: A small weaver with bright red head and breast in breeding males, brown upperparts, and pale underparts. It is endemic to Madagascar and has been introduced to the Comoros, Seychelles, and Mascarene Islands.

children.name: Foudia eminentissima
children.description: A fody with a red head and breast in breeding males, brown upperparts, and pale underparts. It is found on the Comoros Islands and Mayotte.

children.name: Foudia albicans
children.description: A fody with a red head and breast, white belly, and brown upperparts. It is endemic to the mountains of northern Madagascar.

children.name: Foudia flavicans
children.description: A fody with bright yellow underparts and head, olive-green upperparts, and a red bill. It is endemic to the island of Rodrigues.

children.name: Foudia rubra
children.description: A fody with a red head and breast, brown upperparts, and pale underparts. It is endemic to Mauritius and is considered endangered due to habitat loss and introduced predators.

children.name: Foudia sechellarum
children.description: A fody with a yellow-olive body, pale underparts, and a red bill. It is endemic to the Seychelles and is considered vulnerable.

children.name: Textor
children.description: A genus of forest-dwelling weavers with black and yellow plumage, found in Central and West Africa.

children.name: Textor capitalis
children.description: A forest weaver with a black head, yellow body, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Textor albiventris
children.description: A forest weaver with a black head, white belly, yellow breast, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of West Africa.

children.name: Sitagra
children.description: A genus of bright yellow weavers with black or olive heads, found in wetlands and savannas of Africa.

children.name: Sitagra flavoviridis
children.description: A weaver with yellow-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a black bill. It is found in marshes and wetlands of central Africa.

children.name: Sitagra luteola
children.description: A small weaver with yellow underparts, olive upperparts, and a pale bill. It is found in wetlands and grasslands of western Africa.

children.name: Melanoplectus
children.description: A genus of predominantly black weavers with red or yellow markings, found in forests of Central Africa.

children.name: Melanoplectus nigerrimus
children.description: A glossy black weaver with a pale eye, found in lowland forests of central and western Africa.

children.name: Melanoplectus albinuchus
children.description: A black weaver with a white nape patch, found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Oriolops
children.description: A genus of weavers with bright yellow and black plumage resembling orioles, found in forests of Africa.

children.name: Oriolops aurantius
children.description: A brilliant orange-and-black weaver found in mangroves and coastal forests of West Africa.

children.name: Oriolops sulphureus
children.description: A weaver with sulphur-yellow underparts, black head, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Hyphantornis
children.description: A genus of masked weavers with black face masks and bright yellow bodies, found across Africa.

children.name: Hyphantornis velatus
children.description: A masked weaver with black face, yellow body, and olive back. It is common across southern Africa.

children.name: Hyphantornis nigriceps
children.description: A masked weaver with a black head, yellow body, and olive back. It is found in savannas of East Africa.

children.name: Hyphantornis taenioptera
children.description: A masked weaver with a black face mask, yellow body, and brown back. It is found in the Sahel region of Africa.

children.name: Sycobrotus
children.description: A genus of small weavers with slender bills, found in forests and wetlands of Africa.

children.name: Sycobrotus pelzelni
children.description: A small weaver with a slender bill, yellow underparts, olive upperparts, and a black face mask. It is found in marshes of western and central Africa.

children.name: Sycobrotus xanthopterus
children.description: A weaver with a brown throat, yellow body, and olive back. It is found in wetlands of southern Africa.

children.name: Nelicurvius
children.description: A genus of weavers found on the Mascarene Islands, with red and black plumage in males and brown in females.

children.name: Nelicurvius flavicans
children.description: A small fody with yellow underparts, olive upperparts, and a red bill. It is endemic to Rodrigues Island.

children.name: Nelicurvius ruber
children.description: A small fody with red head and breast in males, brown upperparts, and a red bill. It is endemic to Madagascar.

children.name: Ploceus
children.description: Additional species of typical weavers from Africa and Asia, known for their bright breeding plumages and woven nests.

children.name: Ploceus vitellinus
children.description: A masked weaver with a black face, bright yellow body, and olive back. It is found in savannas of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Ploceus flavovirens
children.description: A weaver with yellow-green upperparts, bright yellow underparts, and a black bill. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus flavipes
children.description: A weaver with yellow legs, olive upperparts, and yellow underparts. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus princeps
children.description: A weaver with a black head, yellow body, and olive back. It is endemic to the island of Principe in the Gulf of Guinea.

children.name: Ploceus melanoxanthus
children.description: A weaver with a black head and breast, yellow body, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus nigriventris
children.description: A weaver with a black belly, yellow breast and throat, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus rufaxillaris
children.description: A weaver with rufous wing patches, yellow body, and olive back. It is found in savannas of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus fuscus
children.description: A dark weaver with brownish-black plumage and a pale eye. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus melanospiza
children.description: A weaver with a black bill, yellow body, and olive back. It is found in marshes of East Africa.

children.name: Ploceus cinnabarinus
children.description: A weaver with a bright cinnabar-red breast and belly, black head, and olive back. It is found in marshes of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus aurantiacus
children.description: A weaver with an orange back and rump, black head, and yellow underparts. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus chrysopterus
children.description: A weaver with golden-yellow wing patches, black head, and yellow body. It is found in wetlands of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus erythrops
children.description: A weaver with a red face and throat, yellow body, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of West Africa.

children.name: Ploceus sulfuraceus
children.description: A weaver with sulphur-yellow underparts, olive upperparts, and a black bill. It is found in wetlands of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus luteolus
children.description: A small weaver with yellow underparts, olive upperparts, and a pale bill. It is found in savannas of West Africa.

children.name: Ploceus micropteryx
children.description: A weaver with a black head, yellow body, and relatively small wings. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus albiventris
children.description: A weaver with a black head, white belly, yellow breast, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus flavicans
children.description: A weaver with a yellow back and rump, black head, and yellow underparts. It is found in savannas of central Africa.

children.name: Ploceus castaneiceps
children.description: A weaver with a chestnut crown, black face, yellow body, and olive back. It is found in lowland forests of East Africa.
